g.text: Area Between a Curve and a Line
1. SKETCH the curve and line (even a rough sketch helps)
2. FIND intersection points: solve f(x) = g(x) → these are your limits
3. IDENTIFY which function is on top between the limits
4. SUBTRACT lower from upper: ∫ₚq [f(x) − g(x)] dx
5. INTEGRATE and evaluate — the answer is always positive
Exam tip: If your answer is negative, you probably subtracted in the wrong order.
